Configure Cortex XDR Analytics

From the Cortex XDR management console you can configure analytics engines to detect threats across your environment. The following sections describe the prerequisites and the workflow for enabling each engine. Make sure every prerequisite is satisfied before you turn on the engines, otherwise detection coverage will be partial and noisy.

Identity Analytics prerequisites

Before you activate Identity Analytics, confirm the following items are in place. Each prerequisite ensures the engine has the telemetry it needs to score user behaviour reliably across endpoints, cloud accounts and on-prem directory services.

  • Cortex XDR Analytics must be activated for your tenant.

  • Cloud Identity Engine must be set up. For more information, see Cloud Identity Engine.

Agentic Assistant access

To manage agents in the Agents Hub, you must have the proper permissions. For more information, see Agentic Assistant role-based access control.

Logging notes

Adding Windows DHCP logs can enhance the Analytics Engine. For more information, see Ingest Windows DHCP Logs with an XDR Collector Profile.

After every prerequisite is satisfied, navigate to the Analytics settings panel and enable each engine in turn. Creating a baseline can take up to three hours, during which detection coverage ramps up gradually as the engines learn what normal activity looks like in your environment.
